title: English muffins
subtitle: Yeasted griddle bread with nooks and crannies
excerpt: Soft, yeasted rounds cooked on a dry griddle rather than in the oven, giving a pale, floury exterior and a soft, open crumb full of nooks. Split with a fork, not a knife, to preserve the holes. Toast and butter while still warm.
sourceNotes: Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: muffins described as a griddle bread, cooked over a fire on a griddle or hot stone rather than in an oven. The split-and-toast method is specified. The enriched dough version (with milk and butter) became the standard from the late nineteenth century.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze split muffins. Toast directly from frozen.
recipe.batchNotes: Doubles cleanly — cook in two batches.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Best eaten the day they are made. Freeze any surplus immediately for best results.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: cooked on a dry griddle or heavy frying pan over medium-low heat

glossaryTerms.definition: Cooking on a dry, preheated flat surface — a cast-iron griddle, a heavy frying pan, or a flat skillet — with no oil. For English muffins, the griddle heat sets the base and sides without browning too quickly; the interior cooks by the time the outside is pale gold. Medium-low heat is essential: too high and the outside colours before the inside is cooked through.

content.text: English muffins are cooked on a griddle, not in an oven. The

gives the pale, floury sides and the soft, springy crumb. The characteristic nooks and crannies come from how the muffin is opened: always split with a fork, tearing apart rather than cutting, which tears the internal structure open rather than compressing it.

Combine the {{strong-white-flour}}, {{fine-sea-salt}}, {{caster-sugar}}, and {{instant-yeast}} in a large bowl. Add the warm {{whole-milk}}, {{water}}, and melted {{unsalted-butter}}. Mix to a soft, slightly sticky dough. Knead for 8–10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Shape into a ball, place in an oiled bowl, and cover. Leave at room temperature for 1.5 hours until roughly doubled.

Dust a work surface with {{fine-semolina}}. Roll the dough to 2.5 cm thick. Cut rounds with a 9 cm cutter — press straight down, do not twist. Place on a semolina-dusted tray and cover loosely. Leave for 30 minutes until slightly puffed.
Cook on the griddle, 5–6 minutes each side

Heat a dry, heavy frying pan or griddle over medium-low heat. Cook the muffins in batches for 5–6 minutes per side until pale gold and the sides spring back when pressed. The interior temperature should reach 90°C. Cool on a rack for at least 5 minutes before splitting with a fork.
